data.title: Rajeev Thakkar on PPFAS MF’s cash position: ‘Not selling more than buying’
data.description:
Rajeev Thakkar, CIO, PPFAS MF

The latest slew of portfolio disclosures of around 12 Flexi Cap funds revealed that one of India's marquee value investing fund house PPFAS MF held 20 percent of its entire portfolio as cash in February, up 8 percent on month. It was not just PPFAS that saw an increase in cash holdings, other prominent names like Samir Arora-backed Helios Capital, Axis MF and DSP MF also witnessed a similar trend, sparking concerns of faltering sentiment amid high market volatility.

However, in an interaction with CNBC-TV18,  PPFAS'c chief investment officer, Rajeev Thakkar explained that the increase in cash holdings in February compared to late 2024 was primarily due to market dynamics.

"When equity prices decline, the value of cash and money market instruments remains stable, making their percentage of the portfolio appear higher," Thakkar explained.

Moreover, Thakkar further went on to reassure investors that this shift was not a result of increased selling. "It's not that we have been selling more than we have been buying. On a net basis most days we are buyers in the market but given that opportunities are tough to come by there has been some build up in cash," Thakkar added.

Meanwhile, speaking at Moneycontrol's Global Wealth Summit last month, Rajeev Thakkar expressed strong confidence in private sector lenders for 2025, highlighting their favourable valuations and resilient asset quality.

Beyond banking, Thakkar noted that the market presents a sharp contrast—while some sectors offer compelling investment opportunities, others appear significantly overvalued.

“The key is selectivity,” he emphasised. “There are pockets of deep value and areas of excess froth. The challenge for investors is to distinguish between the two.”

data.title: Nifty Midcap extends rally for a fifth session to gain 8% this week, strength likely to continue
data.description:
Stocks

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of midcap companies extended their rally on March 21 to push the Nifty Midcap index up by over a percent around 51,751, extending gains for a fifth session and rising nearly 8 percent this week.

Strong investor interest has meant the midcap index has outperformed the benchmark Nifty 50 index, which is up nearly 4 percent for the week at above 23,300.

Angel One's Sameet Chavan is hopeful of the outperformance to continue. “Traders are advised to maintain a positive bias toward the midcap space for continued strength,”  he said. Siddhartha Khemka from Motilal Oswal Financial Services said, "…we expect the market recovery to continue in the near term, driven by continued buying interest and positive global cues."

The strength in equities has also coincided with a rise in Rupee, which has extended its rise to an eighth session, exceeding its longest gaining streak in 14 months.

Mankind Pharma shares were the top gainer on the index, jumping over 5 percent to trade at Rs 2,365 apiece. This comes as the stock sees heavy trading volumes, higher than its 10-day average. The pharma stock was followed by Tata Tech, which also surged over 5 percent to trade at Rs 701 per share. The rise in Tata Tech's stock price comes amid reports that it has emerged as one of the key suppliers to Elon Musk's Tesla, as it explores India expansion.

IT major Mphasis rallied nearly 5 percent to trade at Rs 2,416 per share. This comes despite a muted trend in the Nifty IT index, after global information technology player Accenture's second quarter revenue guidance fell short of investor expectations.

Other notable stocks which recorded strong gains included JSW Infra (nearly 5 percent higher), Sundaram Finance (nearly 4 percent higher), Aditya Birla Capital (nearly 4 percent higher), Poonawalla Fincorp (nearly 4 percent higher), Nykaa (nearly 4 percent higher), IREDA (nearly 3 percent higher), BSE (nearly 3 percent higher), Vodafone Idea (nearly 3 percent higher), Coforge (nearly 3 percent higher), Godrej Properties (nearly 3 percent higher), Paytm (nearly 3 percent higher) and more.

data.title: Unicommerce eSolutions fully acquires Shipway Tech to ramp up e-commerce and logistics play
data.description:
Unicommerce eSolutions shares rise with successful completion of acquiring Shipway Tech

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Unicommerce eSolutions shares were higher by over 2 percent after successfully completing the acquisition of 100 percent stake in Shipway Technology, turning it into a subsidiary, in an effort to enhance presence in e-commerce and logistics space.

As of 1:00 on, the shares of Unicommerce eSolutions are trading at Rs 123 with a gain of 2.45 percent.

READ MORE with LIVE updates and news stories

Unicommerce eSolutions completed the acquisition of the remaining 57.24 percent stake in Shipway Technology, making it a wholly-owned subsidiary, after having previously acquired a 42.76 percent stake in December 2024 for Rs 68.4 crore.

The acquisition will be executed through a preferential issue of over 60 lakh equity shares at a share swap ratio of 1:8.9, after the Board approved the transaction on March 20, subject to shareholder nod.

Shipway, a logistics management platform, processes 35 million+ annual transactions and operates across 29,000 pin codes. It is integrated with 15+ courier partners, offering automated shipping solutions. The deal expands Unicommerce’s presence in e-commerce enablement technology, adding logistical capabilities to its existing solutions in inventory, warehouse, and order management.

The combined client base now spans 7,000+ businesses and 10,000+ brands, with only 5% client overlap, presenting cross-sell opportunities. The acquisition is expected to strengthen Unicommerce’s position in the e-commerce technology sector.

Unicommerce eSolutions also acknowledged the resignations of key senior management personnel including Prateek Mahajan (Director - Operations), Pranay Kale (Head of India - Sales), and Vaibhav Malhotra (Senior Director - Marketing), during the board meeting. The company provides end-to-end e-commerce enablement solutions, covering inventory management, order processing, warehouse operations, and returns management through its Uniware platform.

Shipway, its newly acquired subsidiary, specializes in courier aggregation and shipping automation, handling over 35 million transactions annually across 29,000 pin codes. Post-acquisition, Unicommerce's combined client base now spans over 7,000 businesses and 10,000+ brands, with a minimal client overlap, creating strong cross-selling opportunities.

data.title: SEBI undertaking nationwide survey to enhance investor awareness; to come out with an outreach programme
data.description:
SEBI

The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) is conducting a comprehensive nationwide survey to gain deeper insights into investor behavior and address key challenges hindering broader participation in the financial markets, SEBI's Whole-time Member Ananth Narayan said on March 21. The results of the survey are expected to be ready by July.

This initiative involves collaboration with major market infrastructure institutions.

"We are conducting a nationwide survey, using a very large agency along with our market infrastructure institutions to gather insights into two key areas," Narayan said. "The first question is, what does it take to get the 13 crore investors we have to increase even further? Is it a lack of awareness, access, or perhaps fear," he added while speaking at ARIA (Association of Registered Investment Advisors) Aspire 2025 event.

The survey also aims to explore how well current investors grasp critical concepts such as risk, volatility, and asset allocation, as well as how SEBI can more effectively deliver education on these topics.

The survey is expected to be completed by June or July 2025, with SEBI planning a systematic investor outreach program afterward. "The new chairperson has endorsed the need to go through this particular process," Narayan added. "We will use the survey results to push across tailored messages and enhance both investor awareness and risk education," he said.

"Since October 2024, SEBI has worked with social media companies to take down over 70,000 misleading handles," he said, proposing investors use the UPI pay-right handle to identify registered entities, and ensure investors make payments only to Sebi-registered entities. "If we can create this safe space, it will do so much to allow us to filter out the riffraff, and ensure that we do all the good things required for this gated safe community," Ananth Narayan further said.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Indian IT’s discretionary revival hopes fade post Accenture results; Fed spending cuts not alarming
data.description:
Given that a substantial portion of Accenture's workforce is based in India, its results often serve as an indicator of the broader trends.


World’s largest IT services company Accenture’s latest results have dashed all hopes of a discretionary spending revival for Indian IT companies, with clients holding off on new projects amid geopolitical uncertainty.

However, concerns around US federal government spending cuts impacting revenues are minimal for Indian IT firms due to their limited exposure to the federal sector, experts said. Accenture’s revenue from federal services accounted for 8 percent of the company’s global revenue and 16 percent of its American revenue in FY24. The Trump administration’s focus on improving efficiency across federal agencies has led to a slowdown in new procurement actions, directly impacting Accenture’s sales.

Given that a substantial portion of Accenture's workforce is based in India, its results often serve as an indicator of the broader trends and potential outcomes within the $283 billion and over-five-million-strong Indian IT sector.

To put the concerns in the words of Chief Executive Officer Julie Sweet: “We are seeing an elevated level of what was already significant uncertainty in the global economic and geopolitical environment, marking a shift from our Q1FY25 earnings report in December.”

Accenture follows a September-August financial year.

For context, the third quarter of FY25 (Q3FY25) ended December 31, 2024, saw the healthiest recovery for the IT services sector, with companies reporting their strongest recovery in 18 months.

As a result of the commentary from the Dublin-based company, Accenture's share price hit its lowest level since June last year, while the domestic Nifty IT index dropped over 2.5 percent in opening trade.

Discretionary Spending: Hopes DashedHopes of a discretionary spending revival for Indian IT firms have been dashed after Accenture’s latest results. In the past two quarters, there were early signs of discretionary budgets returning, with expectations of a recovery in certain industries such as BFSI.

Nevertheless, trade tensions and uncertainty about policies under the new US administration have kept enterprises focused on business-as-usual (BAU) engagements, delaying new projects.

Phil Fersht, CEO of consulting firm HFS Research, said that Accenture is being cautious in this volatile environment. “The impact of tariffs and uncertainty is delaying some contract decisions as enterprise leaders wait for the current macro situation to calm down,” Fersht told Moneycontrol.

This caution might lead to fewer new discretionary deals, impacting growth prospects for Indian IT companies.

Meanwhile, Accenture raised the lower end of its annual revenue forecast on March 20, despite growing demand for its services to help clients integrate AI-powered tools into their operations.

This is evident from the fact that new bookings in Generative AI (Gen AI) now contribute $1.4 billion, or 7 percent, of the company’s $20.9 billion in new bookings for Q2FY25.

“There was an expectation of discretionary spending increasing (especially in the US) with the new administration coming in. However, the uncertainty created by tariffs and other issues has resulted in organizations continuing to hold back on that,” said Rajesh Ranjan, managing partner at consulting firm Everest Group.

Nonetheless, one glimmer of hope is from the BFSI sector which can be inferred from Accenture's results. “BFSI is doing well which is a good sign for Indian IT service providers,” said Pareekh Jain, founder and CEO of research and advisory firm EIIRTrend.

Notwithstanding, Accenture's Resource segment is not growing, which means the impact will be felt by Indian IT service providers in the manufacturing and engineering service segment.

US Federal Cuts: Little Impact on Indian IT
Spending cuts by the US Federal government have had a significant impact on Accenture, where nearly 10 percent of its revenue originates from. However, Indian IT firms are largely safe from this impact due to their limited exposure to US federal projects.

Analysts point out that while Big Four consulting firms and Accenture are feeling the heat of federal cuts, Indian IT companies are less affected. “The revenue contribution from the federal government is insignificant for Indian providers, partly due to the complex regulatory environment for foreign providers and also because of their limited investments in public sector subsidiaries and arms in the US,” said Gaurav Parab, principal research analyst at consulting firm NelsonHall.

Other IT analysts echoed similar sentiments.

“Federal spending slowdown will not impact much for Indian IT service providers expect second order impact in healthcare,” Jain added.

Tuck-in Acquisition: Not for Everybody
Accenture has traditionally grown its business through aggressive acquisitions over the past decade, and analysts expect this inorganic growth momentum to continue.

The company has completed nearly 70 acquisitions in the last 24 months.

The acquisitions, past and present, will contribute about 2-4 percent for the fiscal, out of the 5-7 percent growth Accenture expects in FY25.

Julian Herbert, vice president at Everest Group pointed out that growth through tuck-in acquisitions is not for everyone.

In the people-driven IT industry, the mergers and acquisition (M&A) play is dependent on a company’s ability to merge acquired companies not only from an asset or go-to-market perspective but more importantly from a cultural lens and integration of people.

“Accenture has been a prolific acquirer with a matured playbook, and in a smaller more measured way companies like Infosys have also targeted specific capabilities or geographic presence and pulled off successful integrations,” Parab added.

With a sluggish market creating opportunities for M&A, experts believe Indian IT firms may also look at inorganic options, especially in non-US geographies, to de-risk from potential slowdowns.

Therefore, inorganic growth is likely to pick up pace in this climate, Fersht believes.

Headcount Growth Slows
Accenture’s headcount addition dropped to just 2,000 this quarter, down from 24,000 in the previous two quarters, reflecting cautious hiring amid macroeconomic uncertainty.

This signals that Accenture is awaiting clarity on tariffs, federal cost-saving initiatives, and the evolving geopolitical situation in the US, say analysts.

Accenture’s shift toward large and long-term transformation deals from the November 2023 quarter resulted in record bookings, thereby, allowing the company to maintain high utilisation levels.

“February quarter (FQ2 '25) attrition at 13 percent was stable, utilisation was flat at 91%, so a sequential drop in net hiring reflects near-term ramp-ups and probably isn't immediately indicative of a change in demand,” Herbert added.

It is now being increasingly discussed among tech circles that slowing headcount growth is an industry-wide trend due to the adoption of Gen AI.

“Tech services companies are indeed trying to drive greater productivity and delink their revenue from FTE (Full-time equivalent) growth with Gen AI and Agentic AI coming into play. So, we expect lesser FTE addition compared to top-line growth,” according to Ranjan.

FTE measures the total full-time hours worked by all employees.

It also indicates that its work in AI-driven custom and platform app development is leading to improved efficiency amongst current resources, added Parab.

Accenture’s cautious outlook on discretionary spending and slowing headcount growth indicates a wait-and-watch approach in a volatile market for its Indian counterparts.

TVS Motor share price surged in March 21 trade after the company declared an interim dividend for the financial year ending 31st March 2025.

Shares of TVS Motor Company touched an intraday high of Rs 2,444.15 per share on the NSE, rising 4.15 percent. The stock has been gaining for the last five trading sessions and is up nearly 8 percent in the period.

data.title: Va Tech Wabag stock jumps 8% as firm signs non-binding term sheet with Norfund-led consortium for $100-mn investment
data.description:
Va Tech Wabag signs non-binding term sheet with Norfund-led consortium for USD 100 million investment

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

VA Tech Wabag (WABAG) shares gained close to 8 percent on Friday, March 21, early during trade, after notifying of a non-binding term sheet it signed to establish a dedicated Municipal Platform focused on capital projects in the municipal water sector. As of 11:00 am, the shares of VA Tech Wabag were trading at Rs 1,493, a gain of 7.94 percent.

READ MORE with LIVE updates and news stories

The platform will commit up to $100 million in equity investment over a 3 to 5 years of time period and will be developed in collaboration with an Investor Consortium, including Norfund, the Norwegian government’s investment fund, along with two other international investors.

As the Technical Partner, WABAG will provide EPC (Engineering, Procurement, and Construction) and O&M (Operations and Maintenance) services while making a minority investment in line with its asset-light strategy. The platform aims to enhance water infrastructure by leveraging the expertise of WABAG and its partners to support sustainable water management and economic development.

The term sheet outlines the framework for collaboration, marking an initial step toward formalising the partnership. The company stated that further updates would follow as definitive agreements are finalised.

In the exchange filing, S. Varadarajan, Whole-time Director & Chief Growth Officer reflected on the deal saying, "This step of signing the term-sheet with strong global partners to establish the Municipal platform underscores our commitment to addressing global water challenges by leveraging cutting-edge technology and expertise. This initiative reflects our asset-light strategy, emphasizing partnerships that combine WABAG’s technical excellence with the financial strengths of our collaborators to accelerate impactful and sustainable water solutions."

data.title: BHEL shares jump 3% after firm wins Rs 7,500-crore order from Gujarat State Electricity Corporation
data.description:
BHEL

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

The shares of Bharat Heavy Electricals (BHEL) jumped over 3 percent on March 21 after the company announced that it has received a letter of intent (LoI) from the Gujarat State Electricity Corporation (GSECL) for a project worth Rs 7,500 crore. BHEL shares were hovering around Rs 214 apiece in morning trade.

In an exchange filing released on March 21, BHEL said that it would supply boiler, turbine, generator and related items as part of the engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) project for an 800-megawatt extension unit in Gujarat's Tapi district. The contract would also include erections and commissioning works, along with related civil works. BHEL said it had received the LoI on March 20.

The commercial operation following the completion of the initial construction of the project is set to begin within 54 months. BHEL further said that it has won the order through international competitive bidding and the broad consideration for the contract stands at around Rs 7,500 crore, excluding taxes and duties.

BHEL shares have now extended gains for the fifth consecutive session. The stock has now strongly recovered after hitting a 52-week low of Rs 176 per share on March 3. It is, however,  over 36 percent lower than its 52-week high of Rs 335 per share which it had hit in July last year. BHEL shares have now fallen over 22 percent over the last six months.

Also read: Our LIVE updates on the stock market

For the quarter which ended in December, BHEL's net profit surged by 170 percent year-on-year to Rs 124 crore. Its revenue meanwhile grew 32 percent to Rs 7,277 crore. BHEL saw a remarkable 167 percent increase in order inflow, while its order book surged 47 percent year-on-year to hit Rs 1.6 lakh crore in Q3 FY25.

data.title: Signature Global acquires 8.39-acre land along Dwarka Expressway in Gurugram; eyes Rs 3,200-crore revenue
data.description:
Signature Global acquires 8.38-acre land at along Dwarka Expressway in Gurugram; eyes Rs 3,200 crore revenue

Delhi NCR-based listed real estate developer, Signature Global (India) Limited has acquired 8.39 acres of land along Dwarka Expressway for Rs 282.42 crore, the company said.

The sale deed of the land, which is located at Sector 37D in Gurugram, was executed on March 20. The overall developable potential of the acquired land would be approximately 2 million square feet, the company said in statement on March 21.

At 11:40 am, Signature Global (India) was quoting at Rs 1,151.85, up Rs 37.30, or 3.35 percent, on the BSE on March 21.

The company intends to launch a luxury housing project on this land parcel by the end of next financial year (2025-26) and it expects total revenue of around Rs 3,200 crore.

Pradeep Aggarwal, Founder and Chairman, Signature Global, said that Gurugram’s housing market has experienced exceptional growth in recent years, with Dwarka Expressway emerging as India’s fastest-growing real estate corridor.

“Strategically located between Delhi and Gurugram, Dwarka Expressway offers unparalleled access to key business hubs, the airport, and an expanding metro network. Among the standout locations, sector 37D has rapidly become a key investment hotspot. Given the success of our past projects, we are confident that our upcoming development in this sector will receive an overwhelming response upon launch,” he said.

According to a CREDAI-Colliers-Liases Foras report, housing prices here have surged by a record 58 percent YoY along this corridor. Seamless connectivity, robust infrastructure, and strong investment potential are some of the driving factors behind such a price growth along Dwarka Expressway.

Signature Global owns 45.1-acre land bank in Gurugram's Sector 37D, also known as DXP Estate, wherein the company is developing both multi-story and low-rise residential units.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

In the first nine months of FY 2024-25, the company added approximately 2.9 million square feet of saleable area to this location. In September 2024, Signature Global had awarded a Rs 1,144 crore construction contract to Ahluwalia Contracts (India) Ltd. for its flagship residential project “De-luxe DXP” located along the Dwarka Expressway.

The company has so far delivered 13.5 million sq. ft. of housing area and has a robust pipeline of approximately 46 million sq. ft. in its forthcoming and ongoing projects, set for execution over the next 2-3 years.

In Q3FY25 the company achieved pre-sales of about Rs 2,770 crore and recognised Rs 830 crore revenue.
